case_brief.variant_request: Generate isomorphic, easier, and harder follow-up practice variants for x^2 - 5x + 6 = 0.

changes: Coefficients changed from -5 and 6 to -7 and 12 while maintaining monic quadratic form and integer factorability
variants.intent: Practice factoring monic quadratics with two distinct integer roots using the same method as the original problem
variants.coach_note: This problem has the same structure as the original. Find two numbers that multiply to 12 and add to -7.

changes: Smaller coefficients (-3 and 2 instead of -5 and 6) with simpler integer roots
variants.intent: Practice factoring very simple monic quadratics with minimal calculation
variants.coach_note: This quadratic factors easily. Look for two numbers that multiply to 2 and add to -3.

design_notes: Preserved core quadratic equation knowledge point throughout all variants
Isomorphic variant maintains identical solution method (factoring) with adjusted coefficients
Easier variant reduces cognitive load through smaller numbers while keeping factorability
Harder variant increases difficulty by requiring quadratic formula due to irrational roots
